SEARCH
Package com.example.moogerscouncil

Class UserRepository

java.lang.Object
com.example.moogerscouncil.UserRepository
public class UserRepository extends Object
Repository for the Firestore 'users' collection. All reads and writes to user documents flow through this class. Activities depend on this class, never on FirebaseFirestore directly.

Follows the Repository design pattern to isolate the data layer from the UI layer and keep Activities testable.

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static interface 
    UserRepository.OnRoleFetchedCallback
    Callback for role-only fetch operations used for post-login routing.
    static interface 
    UserRepository.OnUserCreatedCallback
    Callback for user creation operations.
    static interface 
    UserRepository.OnUserFetchedCallback
    Callback for user fetch operations that return a full Student object.
    static interface 
    UserRepository.OnUserNameCallback
    Callback for name-only user lookups.

    void
    createUser(Student student, UserRepository.OnUserCreatedCallback callback)
    Creates a new user document in Firestore under users/{uid}.

    Fetches the Firestore document for the currently authenticated user and deserializes it into a Student object.
    void
    getCurrentUserRole(UserRepository.OnRoleFetchedCallback callback)
    Resolves the role of the currently authenticated user by checking both Firestore collections: users/{uid} — students and admins; role is stored in the role field. counselors/{uid} — counselors provisioned directly in this collection (no role field required; presence in the collection implies counselor role).
    void
    getUserName(String uid, UserRepository.OnUserNameCallback callback)
    Fetches only the name field for a given user UID.

  • Constructor Details

    • UserRepository

      public UserRepository()
      Constructs a UserRepository backed by the live Firestore instance.

  • Method Details

    • createUser

      public void createUser(Student student, UserRepository.OnUserCreatedCallback callback)
      Creates a new user document in Firestore under users/{uid}. Uses the Student's UID as the document ID so it matches the Firebase Auth UID for easy lookup.
      Parameters:
      student - The Student object to persist.
      callback - Success/failure callback.

    • getCurrentUser

      public void getCurrentUser(UserRepository.OnUserFetchedCallback callback)
      Fetches the Firestore document for the currently authenticated user and deserializes it into a Student object.
      Parameters:
      callback - Receives the Student on success, or an Exception on failure.

    • getUserName

      public void getUserName(String uid, UserRepository.OnUserNameCallback callback)
      Fetches only the name field for a given user UID. Used by AppointmentAdapter to display student names on counselor dashboard appointment cards.

      On Firestore failure, the callback still receives "Unknown" rather than propagating an error — student name is supplementary display data and should never block rendering an appointment card.

      Parameters:
      uid - The Firebase Auth UID of the user to look up.
      callback - Receives the name string on success, or "Unknown" on failure.

    • getCurrentUserRole

      public void getCurrentUserRole(UserRepository.OnRoleFetchedCallback callback)
      Resolves the role of the currently authenticated user by checking both Firestore collections:
      1. users/{uid} — students and admins; role is stored in the role field.
      2. counselors/{uid} — counselors provisioned directly in this collection (no role field required; presence in the collection implies counselor role).

      If the UID is found in neither collection, onFailure is called with an IllegalArgumentException so the caller can display a user-readable error.

      Parameters:
      callback - Receives the role string on success, or an Exception on failure.
